diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Coronary artery disease, chronic kidney disease, history of right lower extremity deep venous thrombosis, thrombocytopenia, hypothyroidism, GERD, rheumatoid arthritis
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with worsening fatigue and dyspnea, suggestive of heart failure exacerbation. Previous medical history included nonischemic cardiomyopathy and severely reduced left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF of 20%). Clinical findings such as significant bilateral lower leg edema, elevated BNP levels, and imaging demonstrating cardiomegaly without acute cardiopulmonary complications supported the diagnosis of acute decompensated systolic heart failure.
